title: Evangelion 20 Being Released On Demand Before DVD
display_title: Evangelion 20 Being Released On Demand Before DVD
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 2.0 Being Released On-Demand Before DVD             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Jan 05, 2011 12:00 pm Same with Summer Wars .

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 05, 2011 12:28 pm So I guess this is specifically 2.0 and not 2.22 then? Interesting news, hopefully 1.0 will be given the same treatment so people wouldn't be horribly confused when downloading it.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Wed Jan 05, 2011 1:49 pm Potential for a high quality rip of the 2.0 cut? I'm game. The changes is 2.22 were atrocious.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Jan 05, 2011 1:56 pm Clover wrote: Potential for a high quality rip of the 2.0 cut? I'm game. The changes is 2.22 were atrocious.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Jan 05, 2011 2:40 pm Have we hit such a low we're now bitching about the extended cuts of Rebuild ruining the original film? I could understand that if the changes added were drastic and screwed around with character logic, theme, and the soul of the film itself (coughDonnieDarkoExtendedCutcough). But the extended cuts of Rebuild are just little fun gifts to the fans. They don't affect the film at all. Well, we all gotta bitch about something. Case in point, I'm bitching about the bitching.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Jan 05, 2011 3:54 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: So I guess this is specifically 2.0 and not 2.22 then? Interesting news, hopefully 1.0 will be given the same treatment so people wouldn't be horribly confused when downloading it.

[]: Consider this puzzle solved.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Wed Jan 05, 2011 4:03 pm Huh... now that's genuinely surprising. I was sure that the anime biz had given up on Video on Demand as a medium a couple years ago. Pretty cool, although the BD will obviously be a higher quality than anything you can get on television. Clover wrote: Potential for a high quality rip of the 2.0 cut? I'm game. The changes is 2.22 were atrocious.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Jan 05, 2011 4:12 pm Made a news post about this yesterday. NAveryW wrote: FUNimation acquired the 2.22 version, but is calling it 2.0 for marketing purposes.

[]: Funimation: "Just as planned."             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Wed Jan 05, 2011 4:17 pm Ornette wrote: That's not what Funimation seems to be claiming: http://blog.funimation.com/2011/01/asian-media-rights-and-funimation-entertainment-agree-to-video-on-demand-partnership/

[]: Re: Funimation: "Just as planned."             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Wed Jan 05, 2011 4:53 pm I posted this yesterday in the Official rebuild dub thread before Ornette made the post in the news section. Killer Bee wrote: Yeah, they specifically mention 2.22 as a separate entity at the end there. I'm guessing they chose to put 2.0 on cable so that you still have to go out and buy 2.22.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Thu Jan 06, 2011 12:08 am So what's the ETA? Does anyone know whether 2.0 will be available on demand by around the time it starts showing in theaters or will they wait until sometime in March, just before the release of the DVD/BluRay?

[]:             Quote Post by Hawq » Sun Jan 09, 2011 7:10 am Am I alone in hoping this kind of thing eventually leads to a set with all the versions of the new films in I wonder? (yes I'm aware that would probably be seen as milking though I lack the Unit 1 milking image to go with it) also shame we don't have anything like that over here :( lucky Americans

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Jan 10, 2011 3:31 am Why would anyone want to own/watch anything BUT 1.11 and 2.22?

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Mon Jan 10, 2011 5:08 am Because they're very strange people. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Mon Jan 10, 2011 6:42 am COACH wrote: Because they're very strange people. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Tue Jan 11, 2011 4:29 am What does that even mean?

[]:             Quote Post by Hawq » Tue Jan 11, 2011 10:46 am Warren Peace wrote: Why would anyone want to own/watch anything BUT 1.11 and 2.22?

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Jan 11, 2011 6:05 pm Aside from the new scenes, I don't think it's worth it. There are little tweaks here and there, but nothing noticeable. It's not as if the versions give different impressions, like the various Blade Runners or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Hawq » Thu Jan 13, 2011 2:42 pm As if that would stop it being released & bought in droves, tons of collectors out there just waiting for such a thing, even if in the first they won't be able to see much
